rationale: Nvidia resumed China H20 chip sales as Washington issued new export licenses, Goldman Sachs called the stock 'compelling' at 21.7x after NVDA denied Kyber NVL144 delay reports; stock has lagged AI-chip peers (SOX ETF +59% YTD vs NVDA flat) despite these positive catalysts, offering a reasonable entry.

Gold at $4,082.73/oz (+1.86% today) with JPMorgan and Bank of America projecting $5,000/oz by Q4 2026 on Fed easing bias, central-bank buying, and cooling June CPI (3.5% YoY, down from 4.2%); establishes initial commodities exposure per policy diversification guidance.

Fed-funds futures now price roughly 53-80% odds of a rate hike (not cut) before year-end 2026 amid sticky inflation and rising Treasury yields (10yr at 4.594%); choosing short-duration SHY over long-duration TLT/BND to gain bond-ETF diversification while minimizing duration risk in a rising-rate environment.
